overview.location: Arlington County, Virginia — directly across the Potomac River from Washington, D.C., 5 miles south of the White House
overview.distinction: Only major airport in the Washington D.C. area with a direct Metro rail connection. Located just 5 miles from the U.S. Capitol and 3 miles from the Pentagon. Subject to unique perimeter rule limiting most nonstop flights to 1,250 miles. River Visual Approach to Runway 19 is one of the most challenging and scenic commercial approaches in the United States — passengers can see the National Mall, Capitol, and Pentagon from the air.
overview.dominantAirline: American Airlines (major hub — operates from Concourses C, D, and E)
overview.renamed: 1998 — renamed to Ronald Reagan Washington National Airport (February 6, 1998)
overview.perimeterRule: 1,250 statute miles — most nonstop flights restricted to destinations within this radius. Beyond-perimeter flights require congressional exemption slots. Originally 650 miles (1966), extended to 1,000 miles (1980s), then 1,250 miles (1986).
overview.beyondPerimeterSlots: 50 daily beyond-perimeter slot exemptions (40 authorized in 2000/2003/2012, plus 10 added by FAA Reauthorization Act of 2024)
overview.metroAccess: Direct Yellow/Blue Line connection via Ronald Reagan Washington National Airport Metro station — covered walkway to Terminal 2
overview.bikeAccess: Mount Vernon Trail connects to airport. 48 bike parking spots. Capital Bikeshare dock-based station (first major U.S. airport with bikeshare).
overview.securityRestriction: Flight Restricted Zone 13-15 nautical miles around airport. General aviation limited to 48 operations/day. Only scheduled commercial and governmental flights permitted without FAA waiver.
overview.environmentalNote: Located on the Potomac River in the flight path of restricted airspace around the National Mall, U.S. Naval Observatory, and White House

terminals.name: Terminal 1 (Gates A1-A9)
terminals.status: Active — 9 gates. Original 1941 terminal designed by Charles M. Goodman. Historic Art Deco architecture.
terminals.notes: Original terminal opened 1941. Southwest Airlines is the predominant carrier. Terminals 1 and 2 are NOT connected post-security — passengers must exit and re-enter security to switch terminals. USO Lounge located pre-security, across from Lost and Found. Nursing rooms available pre-security.

terminals.name: Terminal 2 — Concourse B (Gates B10-B22)
terminals.status: Active — 13 gates. Part of Terminal 2 complex opened July 27, 1997, designed by César Pelli.
terminals.notes: Delta Sky Club above Gate 15. United Club across from Gate 11. Capital One Landing lounge. Metro station connects via covered walkway to Terminal 2.

terminals.name: Terminal 2 — Concourse C (Gates C23-C34)
terminals.notes: American Airlines Admirals Club in Concourse C (hours: Sun-Fri 4:45 AM-10:15 PM, Sat 4:45 AM-9:15 PM). American Express Centurion Lounge past South Security Checkpoint. All post-security concourses B through E in Terminal 2 are connected via National Hall.

terminals.name: Terminal 2 — Concourse D (Gates D35-D45)
terminals.notes: American Airlines Admirals Club in Concourse D. Exclusively American Airlines gates.

terminals.name: Terminal 2 — Concourse E (Gates E46-E59)
terminals.status: Active — 14 gates. Newest concourse, opened April 2021 as part of Project Journey ($1B+). Replaced the notorious Gate 35X bus gates.
terminals.notes: Brand-new concourse with jetbridges (replacing old bus boarding). American Airlines Admirals Club. Mother's care rooms. Indoor pet relief area. Concessions. Amenities are newest in the airport. 14 regional jet gates with jetways.

roads: U.S. Route 1 (Jefferson Davis Highway / Richmond Highway)
transit: WMATA Metrorail Yellow Line (direct — 4 stops to L'Enfant Plaza)
WMATA Metrorail Blue Line (direct — 12 stops to L'Enfant Plaza)
Capital Bikeshare station (dock-based, first at a major U.S. airport)

metro.paymentMethod: SmarTrip card — purchase at fare machines in station or use contactless payment (Apple Pay, Google Pay, contactless credit card)
metro.frequency: Every 6-12 minutes depending on time and day
metro.stationLocation: Adjacent to Terminal 2 — covered moving walkway connects to all airline terminals

travelTimes.toLEnfantPlaza: ~10 minutes (Yellow Line, 4 stops) — near National Mall and Smithsonian museums
travelTimes.toMetroCenter: ~19 minutes (Blue Line) — downtown DC transfer hub
travelTimes.toDupontCircle: ~20 minutes — via transfer at Metro Center
travelTimes.toUnionStation: ~18 minutes — via transfer at Gallery Place (Red Line)
travelTimes.toDullesAirport: ~60 minutes (Silver Line via transfer at Rosslyn)

metro.competitorNote: At $2.25 off-peak, Metro is the cheapest and often fastest way to reach downtown DC. A round-trip Metro fare ($4.50-$13.50) is dramatically cheaper than even one day of airport parking ($25-$29). For trips of any length, Metro beats parking on cost. This is DCA's single biggest competitive advantage over IAD and BWI.
metro.accessibility: Elevator access, wheelchair-accessible platforms, priority seating

metrobus.coverage: Crystal City, Pentagon, Arlington Cemetery, Federal Triangle, downtown Washington D.C.
metrobus.note: Metrobus routes 13F and 13G stop on the upper roadway next to Terminal B. Primarily used on weekend mornings when Metrorail service has not yet started.

taxi.pickup: Designated taxi stands at arrivals level of both terminals. DCA Taxi Dispatcher available to assist.
taxi.meterRate: First mile: $5.39. Each additional mile: $2.16. Airport surcharge: $3.00. Additional passenger: up to $1.00 each.
taxi.dcMaxFare: Trips within District of Columbia have a maximum fare of $19.00
taxi.snowEmergency: Fare increased 125% during declared Snow Emergency
taxi.note: All taxicabs must have and use meters. Cash and credit cards accepted.

pickupLocations.terminal1: Third (outer) curb outside Terminal 1 arrivals. Look for green flag markers.
pickupLocations.terminal2: Baggage Claim (Arrivals) Level, 1st Floor, outer curb. Zones 1-4.

rideshare.surgePricing: Standard surge pricing during peak hours, weather events, and holidays
rideshare.pinVerification: PIN or QR verification may be required
rideshare.competitiveAdvantage: DCA is only 5 miles from downtown DC — rideshare is very affordable. Round-trip Uber at $24-40 beats even 1 day of terminal parking ($29). For solo travelers, Metro at $2.25-$6.75 is even cheaper.

onAirport.location: On-airport rental car counters on first floor of Parking 1 (Terminal 1)
onAirport.shuttle: Airport shuttle to 'Rental Car/Parking' stop at Parking 1. Board at middle curb between Doors 1 and 2 at Terminal 1.

offAirport.shuttle: Dedicated branded shuttle buses between airport and off-site lot. Follow purple signs at Terminal 1 for off-airport rental car shuttles.

rentalCars.note: On-airport companies (Alamo, Dollar, Enterprise, Hertz, National, Sixt, Thrifty) are inside Parking 1. Avis and Budget are off-airport with shuttle service.

groundTransportation.hotelShuttles: Many Crystal City and Pentagon City hotels offer free courtesy shuttles to/from the airport, including Hyatt Regency Crystal City, Crystal Gateway Marriott, Hilton Crystal City, Westin Crystal City, and Sheraton Pentagon City.

parkingRates: On-airport: Parking 1 (Terminal 1 Garage) $35/day, Parking 2 (Terminal 2 Garage) $29/day, Economy Lot $25/day. First 60 minutes FREE in garages. Cell Phone Lot: Temporarily CLOSED (construction). Off-site: from ~$8.50/day (DoubleTree Crystal City, Sheraton Pentagon). Pre-booking strongly recommended — Economy Lot capacity reduced ~40% during new garage construction.

parking1.name: Parking 1 (Terminal 1 Garage)
parking1.rate: $7 per hour after first 60 minutes free
parking1.evCharging: EV charging stations on Level 1, complimentary for parking customers
parking1.features: Closest garage to Terminal 1. First 60 minutes free for pickups/drop-offs. On-airport rental car counters on first floor.

parking2.name: Parking 2 (Terminal 2 Garage)
parking2.rate: $6 per hour after first 60 minutes free
parking2.evCharging: 101 EV charging stations on Level 1, complimentary for parking customers
parking2.features: Adjacent to Terminal 2 and Metro station. First 60 minutes free. Largest garage at DCA.

economyParking.name: Economy Lot
economyParking.capacity: 2,680 total spaces (currently reduced ~40% due to new parking deck construction)
economyParking.construction: New 3-story parking deck being built on top of Economy Lot. Phase 1: 939 spaces opening February 2026. Phase 2: additional 1,230 spaces opening May 2026. Contractor: Kiwi Newton. Cost: ~$40 million.
economyParking.features: Budget-friendly long-term option. Reserve in advance — capacity significantly reduced during construction.

cellPhoneLot.status: TEMPORARILY CLOSED due to Project Journey construction
cellPhoneLot.alternative: Use Parking 1 or Parking 2 garages — first 60 minutes free for passenger pickup
cellPhoneLot.rules: When open: driver must remain with vehicle. For drivers waiting to pick up arriving passengers only.

valetParking.status: NOT AVAILABLE — DCA does not offer on-site valet parking

onAirportParking.totalCapacity: 8,500 vehicles across all DCA parking facilities
onAirportParking.paymentMethods: Credit cards, debit cards accepted. Pre-booking available online at reserve.flyreagan.com.
onAirportParking.freePickupDropoff: First 60 minutes FREE in Parking 1 and Parking 2 garages — use for passenger pickup/drop-off instead of cell phone lot during construction.

parkingLots.name: The Parking Spot (Crystal Gateway Marriott)
parkingLots.shuttleInfo: Shuttle runs every 30 minutes, 5:00 AM - 11:30 PM. May make additional stops en route to airport.
parkingLots.overview: The Parking Spot partners with Crystal Gateway Marriott for secure garage parking at DCA. One of the most affordable off-site options at $9/day. Located 1.25 miles from Reagan National Airport.
parkingLots.address: 1700 Richmond Highway, Arlington, VA 22202 (Crystal Gateway Marriott)
parkingLots.notes: Garage parking (covered). Shuttle NOT 24/7 (5 AM - 11:30 PM). Not suitable for very early/late flights.

parkingLots.name: Sheraton Pentagon City Hotel
parkingLots.shuttleInfo: Complimentary shuttle runs once per hour, 6:00 AM - 11:15 PM.
parkingLots.overview: Budget-friendly hotel parking near Pentagon City Metro station. No hotel stay required. Pre-book to lock in $9/day rate vs $12 walk-up. Close to shops and restaurants at Pentagon City mall.
parkingLots.notes: Shuttle runs once per hour only. NOT 24/7 (6 AM - 11:15 PM). Allow extra time for shuttle wait.

parkingLots.name: Crystal Gateway Marriott
parkingLots.shuttleInfo: Complimentary shuttle every 30 minutes, 5:30 AM - Midnight. Travels to and from Reagan National Airport.
parkingLots.overview: Crystal Gateway Marriott is 1 mile from DCA with covered garage parking. No hotel stay required for parking. Located in Crystal City with restaurants, shops, and Crystal City Metro station nearby.
knownCoupons: Book via parkon.com for competitive rates
parkingLots.notes: Covered garage parking. Convenient Crystal City location near Metro. Shuttle every 30 min. One of the best value DCA off-site options.

parkingLots.name: Renaissance Arlington Capital View
parkingLots.shuttleInfo: Regular free shuttle service to and from DCA included with reservation.
parkingLots.overview: Modern hotel in Crystal City area with parking available to non-guests. Good views and dining options on-site.
parkingLots.notes: Newer hotel with good amenities. Free shuttle included.

parkingLots.name: Hyatt Regency Crystal City
parkingLots.shuttleInfo: Free airport shuttle to Terminal 1 — pickup outside main doors. Estimated times posted at hotel.
parkingLots.overview: Premium Crystal City hotel with covered valet parking. Walking distance to Crystal City Metro. Popular Park-Sleep-Fly option.
parkingLots.notes: Covered valet parking — your car stays in a garage. Higher rate but premium service. Close to Metro if you prefer train to shuttle.

parkingLots.name: The Westin Crystal City (DCA)
parkingLots.shuttleInfo: Complimentary airport shuttle service to and from DCA. No hotel reservation required to park.
parkingLots.overview: Closest off-site lot to DCA at just 0.8 miles. Covered garage parking in a full-service Westin hotel. Free shuttle included. No hotel stay required. Walking distance to Crystal City Metro.
parkingLots.notes: Closest off-site option. Covered parking is a differentiator — most DCA off-site lots are uncovered. $12.90/day is competitive for covered.

parkingLots.name: Sheraton Suites Old Town Alexandria (DCA)
parkingLots.shuttleInfo: Complimentary shuttle to DCA. Limited availability — book early.
parkingLots.overview: Budget option in Old Town Alexandria, 2.6 miles from DCA. One of the cheapest off-site DCA parking options at $8.50/day. Hotel amenities available. Wheelchair accessible.
parkingLots.address: 801 N Saint Asaph St, Alexandria, VA 22314
parkingLots.notes: Budget option but limited availability — often sells out. Furthest off-site lot at 2.6 mi but cheapest at $8.50/day.

parkingLots.name: DoubleTree by Hilton Crystal City
parkingLots.overview: Budget-friendly option in Crystal City. One of the cheapest off-site DCA parking options when pre-booked. No hotel stay required.
parkingLots.notes: Best budget option for DCA off-site parking. Pre-book to secure lowest rate.

parkingLots.name: Hilton Crystal City at Washington Reagan National Airport
parkingLots.shuttleInfo: Free roundtrip airport shuttle service included with parking.
parkingLots.overview: Full-service Hilton in Crystal City with airport parking for non-guests. Popular Park-Sleep-Fly hotel. Walking distance to Crystal City Metro.
parkingLots.notes: Good balance of price and amenities. Walking distance to Crystal City Metro for downtown DC access.

shuttleInfo: Most off-site hotel lots include free shuttle to DCA. Frequency varies: Crystal Gateway Marriott (every 30 min, 5:30 AM-midnight), Sheraton Pentagon (hourly, 6 AM-11:15 PM), Hyatt Regency (on schedule), Westin (on demand). Economy Lot has its own shuttle. NOTE: Most hotel shuttles are NOT 24/7 — check hours for early morning/late night flights. Metro is the backup: Crystal City station is walking distance from most hotels, Yellow/Blue Line direct to airport.
distanceFromCity: 5 miles south of downtown Washington D.C. across the Potomac River. 10-20 minutes by car depending on traffic. 10 minutes by Metro Yellow Line. The closest major airport to the National Mall, White House, and Capitol.

keyDistances.toDowntownDC: 5 miles, 10-20 min by car, 10 min by Metro (Yellow Line), $12-20 rideshare
keyDistances.toCapitolHill: 4.5 miles, 10-18 min by car, 15 min by Metro, $12-18 rideshare
keyDistances.toGeorgetown: 6 miles, 15-25 min by car (no direct Metro), $15-22 rideshare
keyDistances.toNationalMall: 5 miles, 10-20 min by car, 10 min by Metro to L'Enfant Plaza, $12-20 rideshare
keyDistances.toPentagon: 1.5 miles, 3-5 min by car, 3 min by Metro (1 stop), $8-12 rideshare
keyDistances.toCrystalCity: 1 mile, 3 min by car, 2 min by Metro, $6-10 rideshare
keyDistances.toTysonsCorner: 13 miles, 20-40 min by car, ~40 min by Metro (Silver Line transfer), $25-40 rideshare
keyDistances.toDullesAirport: 27 miles, 30-50 min by car, ~60 min by Metro (Silver Line transfer)
keyDistances.competitiveAdvantage: DCA is the closest airport to downtown DC (5 miles). Metro Yellow Line reaches L'Enfant Plaza in 10 minutes for $2.25-$6.75. For solo travelers, a round-trip Metro fare ($4.50-$13.50) costs less than one hour of garage parking. DCA's direct Metro connection is unmatched — IAD (Dulles) requires a 60+ minute Metro ride, and BWI requires MARC Train or Amtrak.

construction2026.status: Project Journey complete (April 2021). DCA Reimagined in progress. Economy Lot parking deck under construction.
projects: Project Journey ($1B+) — COMPLETE (April 2021). New Concourse E (14 gates replacing Gate 35X bus boarding), two new 50,000 sq ft security checkpoint buildings, National Hall expansion for post-security dining/shopping.
DCA Reimagined — IN PROGRESS (multi-year). New concessions, restroom upgrades, new amenities throughout Terminal 2. Goal: modernize passenger experience.

Economy Lot Parking Deck ($40M) — UNDER CONSTRUCTION. New 3-story parking deck built on top of Economy Lot. Phase 1: 939 spaces (February 2026). Phase 2: 1,230 additional spaces (May 2026). Contractor: Kiwi Newton. Up to 1,100 of 2,680 Economy Lot spaces unavailable during peak construction.
Cell Phone Lot — TEMPORARILY CLOSED during construction. Use Parking 1/2 garages (first 60 min free) for pickups.
construction2026.travelImpact: Economy Lot capacity reduced ~40% during parking deck construction. Reserve parking in advance. Cell phone lot closed — use garage first-60-minutes-free option instead.

timeline.economyDeckPhase2: May 2026 — 1,230 additional spaces (total ~2,169 new structured spaces)
timeline.dcaReimagined: Multi-year — ongoing concession and amenity upgrades

tsaPreCheck.avgWaitTime: About 10 minutes — roughly half the standard line

clear.note: CLEAR available at Terminal 2 security checkpoints

standardWaitTimes.peakWait: 30-45+ minutes during peak hours, especially Monday mornings and Friday afternoons (congressional travel patterns)
standardWaitTimes.tips: Use MyTSA app for real-time wait times. Monday mornings and Friday afternoons are busiest due to congressional and government commuter traffic.

securityCheckpoints.terminal1: Security checkpoint at Terminal 1 (Gates A1-A9) — NOT connected post-security to Terminal 2
securityCheckpoints.terminal2North: North Security Checkpoint serving Gates B10-E59
securityCheckpoints.terminal2South: South Security Checkpoint serving Gates B10-E59 (Centurion Lounge past this checkpoint)

arrivalRecommendation.domestic: 2 hours before departure (DCA is exclusively domestic/short-haul — no customs/immigration)
arrivalRecommendation.holidays: 2.5-3 hours for Thanksgiving, Christmas, spring break, inauguration events

terminal1.name: USO Lounge
terminal1.location: Terminal 1, pre-security, across from Lost and Found
terminal1.hours: Mon-Fri: 6:00 AM - 6:00 PM, Sat-Sun: 8:00 AM - 4:00 PM
terminal1.access: Active-duty, Guard, Reserve, retired military, and their dependents. Free.
terminal1.amenities: Air conditioning, coffee, tea, snack bar, TV, playing cards, charging stations, computers, printers, WiFi, library, desks/work areas.

terminal2.name: American Airlines Admirals Club (Concourse C)
terminal2.hours: Sun-Fri: 4:45 AM - 10:15 PM, Sat: 4:45 AM - 9:15 PM
terminal2.access: Admirals Club membership, Oneworld Emerald/Sapphire, AAdvantage Executive Platinum/Platinum Pro, Citi/AAdvantage Executive card.
terminal2.amenities: Complimentary food, drinks, full-service bar, WiFi, workspaces, power outlets.

terminal2.name: American Airlines Admirals Club (Concourse D)
terminal2.hours: Sun-Fri: 4:45 AM - 10:15 PM, Sat: 4:45 AM - 9:15 PM
terminal2.access: Same as Concourse C Admirals Club.

terminal2.name: American Airlines Admirals Club (Concourse E)
terminal2.hours: Sun-Fri: 4:45 AM - 10:15 PM, Sat: 4:45 AM - 9:15 PM
terminal2.amenities: Newest Admirals Club at DCA. Complimentary food, drinks, bar, WiFi, workspaces.

terminal2.name: American Express Centurion Lounge
terminal2.location: Terminal 2, past the South Security Checkpoint
terminal2.hours: Sun-Fri: 5:00 AM - 9:00 PM ET, Sat: 5:00 AM - 7:00 PM ET
terminal2.access: Amex Platinum, Centurion, and Business Platinum cardholders. 2 guests included.
terminal2.amenities: Complimentary food (often locally-inspired menu), premium cocktails, wine, beer, WiFi, shower suites, workspaces, phone booths.

terminal2.name: Capital One Landing
terminal2.access: Capital One Venture X Rewards or Venture X Business primary cardholders. Complimentary entry.
terminal2.amenities: Tapas bar, made-to-order food, full bar, restrooms, WiFi, charging stations, luggage nooks.

terminal2.name: Delta Sky Club
terminal2.hours: Sun-Fri: 5:00 AM - 9:30 PM, Sat: 5:00 AM - 7:00 PM
terminal2.access: Delta Sky Club membership, Delta Reserve Amex, Delta One, SkyTeam Elite Plus.
terminal2.amenities: Complimentary food, premium spirits, beer, wine, WiFi, power outlets, shower suites.

terminal2.name: United Club
terminal2.location: Across from Gate 11, Concourse B, Terminal 2
terminal2.access: United Club membership, United Polaris, Star Alliance Gold, United Club Infinite card.

evCharging.totalPorts: 100+ EV charging stations across Parking 1 and Parking 2
evCharging.recommendation: Parking 2 has the most EV chargers (101 stations). Arrive early for charging spots.

luggageStorage.onSite: Baggage storage is NOT available at Reagan National Airport.

offSiteAlternatives.locations: Multiple locations in Crystal City, downtown DC, Union Station

offSiteAlternatives.note: Flexible hourly or daily. Locations near Metro stations.
Near Crystal City and downtown DC

petFacilities.total: 6 pet relief areas across Terminal 1 and Terminal 2
preSecurity: Terminal 1 — Outdoor pet relief areas at north and south ends of Terminal 1 roadway curb
Terminal 2 — Pre-security locations adjacent to North and South Metrorail Station Entrances
postSecurity: Terminal 1 (Gates A1-A9) — Post-security pet relief area at north end of National Hall
Terminal 2 (Concourse E) — Indoor pet relief area near Gates E46-E59
petFacilities.features: Waste bags, receptacles, and clearly marked signage at all locations.

nursingRooms.terminal1: Nursing/lactation rooms available pre-security at Terminal 1
nursingRooms.terminal2: Lactation rooms located between Concourses B and C, post-security. Private rooms with seating, power outlets, and changing tables.
nursingRooms.features: Private rooms with seating, power outlets, changing tables. Look for 'Mother's Care Room' or 'Lactation Room' signage.

peakTravelTimes.congressionalPattern: DCA sees unique weekday traffic spikes from members of Congress, government officials, and lobbyists commuting between DC and their districts. Monday AM and Friday PM are notably busier than peer airports.

arrivalRecommendation.holidays: 2.5-3 hours for Thanksgiving, Christmas/New Year, spring break, Cherry Blossom Festival (late March/early April), Inauguration Day

weatherPatterns.onTimeRate: Departures: ~77% on time, Arrivals: ~81% on time
weatherPatterns.climate: Humid subtropical climate with four distinct seasons. Hot, humid summers. Cold winters with occasional snow/ice. Spring cherry blossom season (late March-early April).
weatherPatterns.commonDisruptions: Summer thunderstorms (June-August), winter snow/ice storms (December-March), Potomac River fog (morning, spring/fall), wind shear from river valley
weatherPatterns.singleRunwayNote: While DCA has 3 runways, Runway 01/19 handles ~57% of operations. Capacity hard-capped at 67 operations/hour by federal regulation — any weather disruption causes cascading delays with no recovery buffer.
weatherPatterns.riverVisualApproach: River Visual Approach to Runway 19 is one of the most challenging in commercial aviation — pilots must follow the Potomac River, banking sharply near the National Mall. This approach can be suspended during low visibility/fog.
weatherPatterns.bestMonths: September through November — mild weather, lower tourist crowds, fewer thunderstorms
weatherPatterns.summerThunderstorms: June-August: late afternoon/evening thunderstorms can cause 1-3 hour ground stops. Very common in DC area. Book morning flights for best reliability.
weatherPatterns.winterWeather: December-March: snow and ice storms can shut down airport for hours. Taxi surcharge of 125% during declared Snow Emergency. Carry overnight bag in winter.
weatherPatterns.springFog: March-May: morning fog from Potomac River can delay early departures. Usually clears by mid-morning.

parkSleepFly.description: Hotel + parking + shuttle packages near DCA in Crystal City/Pentagon City/Arlington. Many hotels offer up to 14 days of free parking with one-night hotel stay.
parkSleepFly.priceRange: From ~$175/night (1-night stay + up to 14 days parking + free airport shuttle)

popularHotels.name: Hilton Crystal City at Reagan National Airport
popularHotels.highlight: Full-service Hilton. Walking distance to Crystal City Metro.

popularHotels.name: Crystal Gateway Marriott
popularHotels.highlight: Covered garage. Also available as parking-only (no hotel stay).

metroRoundTrip.note: Every 6-12 min, direct service. The cheapest option by far.

rideshareRoundTrip.note: Cheaper than ANY parking for trips under 2 days

savingsComparison.keyDifference: DCA's Metro connection is a game-changer. A round-trip Metro fare for 2 people ($9-$27) costs less than ONE day of terminal parking ($29-$35). For travelers coming from anywhere on the Yellow/Blue Line, there is zero reason to pay for parking. Even off-site lots at $8.50/day can't compete with $2.25 Metro fare. The parking vs. transit calculus at DCA is uniquely tilted toward transit.

uniqueFeatures.perimeterRule: Most nonstop flights limited to 1,250 statute miles. This means no direct flights to most West Coast cities (except via limited beyond-perimeter exemption slots). Travelers to LAX, SFO, SEA have limited flight options from DCA. Originally implemented in 1966 at 650 miles to protect Dulles Airport traffic.
uniqueFeatures.slotControls: DCA is one of only 3 slot-controlled airports in the U.S. (along with JFK and LGA). Maximum 67 operations per hour. Airlines must use 80% of slots or lose them. This caps growth and keeps the airport at capacity year-round.
uniqueFeatures.metroConnection: Only major airport in the DC area with direct Metro rail service. Yellow and Blue lines stop at the airport station with covered walkway to terminals. This is DCA's defining competitive advantage over IAD (Dulles) and BWI.
uniqueFeatures.proximityToGovernment: Closest airport to the White House (5 miles), U.S. Capitol (4.5 miles), Pentagon (1.5 miles), Supreme Court, and the National Mall. Makes it the airport of choice for government officials, lobbyists, and political travelers.
uniqueFeatures.riverVisual: The River Visual Approach to Runway 19 requires pilots to follow the Potomac River and bank steeply, offering passengers dramatic views of the National Mall, Washington Monument, Lincoln Memorial, and U.S. Capitol. One of the most famous approaches in commercial aviation.
uniqueFeatures.capitalBikeshare: First major U.S. airport with a dock-based bikeshare station. Mount Vernon Trail connects the airport to Alexandria, the Pentagon, and Arlington via a paved bike/pedestrian path.
uniqueFeatures.dcaVsIadVsBwi: DCA is closest to DC but has the perimeter rule and slot controls. IAD (Dulles) has unlimited long-haul/international flights but is 27 miles west with no direct Metro until Silver Line extension. BWI (Baltimore) has Southwest Airlines' largest DC-area operation but is 42 miles north. For downtown DC trips, DCA + Metro is unbeatable on time and cost.
uniqueFeatures.noInternationalFlights: Due to the perimeter rule, DCA has effectively no long-haul international service. All international traffic for the DC area goes through IAD (Dulles). DCA's 'international' flights are limited to short-haul Canada destinations (Air Canada Express to Toronto, Montreal).
uniqueFeatures.federalPropertyOwnership: Unlike most U.S. airports, DCA is owned by the federal government (not a city or county). It is operated by MWAA under a long-term lease from the federal government.

liveSources._description: Authoritative URLs for article generation. Verified 2026-04-03.
